_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about the three levels of government in Australia — local, state/territory, and federal — and what each one is responsible for.

_source.w: We are learning about the three levels of government in Australia — local, state/territory, and federal — and what each one is responsible for.
y: Knowing which level of government handles which issues helps us direct our questions and concerns to the right place.
Understanding the three levels shows us how responsibilities are shared across the country.
This knowledge prepares us to engage with the government system as active citizens.
eg: Creating a chart that sorts responsibilities like roads, hospitals, schools, and defence into the correct level of government.
Comparing what our local council does with what the state government handles.
Investigating who is responsible for making laws about education in Victoria.
Looking at how the three levels sometimes work together on issues like emergency response.
Discussing why we need three levels instead of just one national government.
Mapping the structure from local council to state parliament to federal parliament.
